storyBeat: 醜小鴨的故事開始前 — 先學關鍵字

questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: Tonight's story has a few new words. Let's learn them first!
questions.sentenceZh: 今晚的故事會用到幾個新字，我們先把它們學會！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：今晚要講醜小鴨的故事，先來認識幾個關鍵字，記住了故事更好聽喔！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese word to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
這四個字都在醜小鴨的故事裡：
🔑 egg = 蛋
🔑 pond = 池塘
🔑 nest = 窩
🔑 swan = 天鵝

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Four more words — can you match them all?
questions.explanationZh:
再來四個：
🔑 ugly = 醜
🔑 feather = 羽毛
🔑 lonely = 孤單
🔑 beautiful = 美麗

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one is an egg?
questions.question: Which one is an egg?
questions.explanationZh: egg = 蛋 🥚！故事裡鴨媽媽守著一窩蛋，最大的那顆一直遲遲沒有孵出來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one shows a swan?
questions.question: Which one shows a swan?
questions.explanationZh: swan = 天鵝 🦢！故事的結局，醜小鴨慢慢長大，竟然變成了一隻美麗的天鵝！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The mother duck sat on her nest beside the pond.
questions.sentenceZh: 鴨媽媽坐在池塘邊的窩上。
questions.question: Which picture matches the sentence?
options: a duck flying high above the clouds
a duck swimming alone in the open sea
a duck sitting on a nest near a calm pond
a duck running quickly across a dry field
optionsZh: 一隻鴨子獨自在大海裡游泳
一隻鴨子坐在平靜池塘邊的窩上
一隻鴨子快速跑過乾燥的田野
questions.explanationZh: pond = 池塘，nest = 窩，句子說鴨媽媽坐在池塘邊的窩上——圖中正是這個畫面。

storyBeat: 再記幾個字，故事就要開始了

questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: A few more words to learn — then the story begins!
questions.sentenceZh: 再記幾個字，故事就要開始了！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：再學幾個重要的字，學會了，故事就更好懂囉！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese phrase to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
這四個片語都出現在醜小鴨的故事裡：
🔑 grow up = 長大
🔑 reflection = 倒影
🔑 cold winter = 冷冷的冬天
🔑 flew away = 飛走了

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The duckling saw his reflection in the water.
questions.sentenceZh: 小鴨在水面看到了自己的倒影。
questions.question: Which picture matches 'a reflection in the water'?
options: a duckling hiding alone under a green bush
a duckling looking at itself in the water
a duckling sleeping inside a warm round nest
a duckling eating small seeds on dry land
optionsZh: 一隻小鴨躲在灌木叢下
一隻小鴨低頭看著水面的倒影
一隻小鴨在溫暖的窩裡睡覺
一隻小鴨在乾地上吃種子
questions.explanationZh: reflection = 倒影，句子說小鴨看著水面上的自己——圖中正是小鴨低頭看水面倒影的樣子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The duckling shivered alone in the cold winter.
questions.question: Which emoji shows winter?
questions.explanationZh: winter = 冬天 ❄️！醜小鴨獨自一人在冰冷的冬天裡發抖，好可憐喔。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The ugly duckling ___ alone all through the cold winter.
questions.sentenceEasy: The duckling ___ alone in winter.
questions.sentenceHard: Through every bitter cold night of the long winter, the ugly duckling ___ quite alone.
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh:
這個句子說的是過去發生的事，live 的過去式是 lived（規則動詞加 -d）。
完整句：The ugly duckling lived alone all through the cold winter.

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Let's review all the new words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
把這節的重點字再複習一遍：
🔑 ugly = 醜
🔑 beautiful = 美麗
🔑 grow up = 長大
🔑 lonely = 孤單

storyBeat: 農場 — 被啄被追 — 媽媽轉頭

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 farmyard = 農場
🔑 hen = 母雞
🔑 peck = 啄
🔑 chase = 追
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Soon the family walked to the farmyard for the first time.
questions.sentenceEasy: The family went to the farmyard.
questions.sentenceHard: Before long, mother duck led her little family on their very first walk to the noisy farmyard.
questions.explanationZh: 鴨媽媽帶著全家，第一次走向農場。嘰嘰嘎嘎的聲音越來越響，這一大家子，要開始新生活了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: There were big hens, loud ducks, and an angry old goose.
questions.sentenceEasy: The farmyard had hens, ducks, and a goose.
questions.sentenceHard: The farmyard was full of life — big hens strutting proudly, ducks quacking loudly, and one bad-tempered old goose who glared at every newcomer.
questions.explanationZh: 農場好熱鬧！大母雞昂首闊步，鴨子嘎嘎亂叫，還有一隻老鵝，瞇著眼睛，對所有新來的傢伙都很兇。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: All the animals turned their eyes to the large grey baby.
questions.questionEn: Did the animals welcome him kindly?
questions.explanationZh: 所有動物都齊刷刷地盯過來看，那不是歡迎，是「審視」。被這樣看著，哪裡舒服得了？所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有的動物都把眼睛轉向那個又大又灰的寶寶。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: All the animals turned their eyes to the large grey baby when the family arrived at the farmyard.
questions.sentenceZh: 當全家走進農場，所有動物都把眼睛轉向那個又大又灰的寶寶。
questions.question: What happened when the grey duckling arrived at the farmyard?
questions.explanationZh: 所有動物齊刷刷地轉頭看他——不是歡迎，是「盯著他看」，讓人渾身不自在。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A red hen ran toward him with her wings spread wide.
questions.sentenceEasy: A red hen ran at him.
questions.sentenceHard: Without warning, a large red hen came charging toward him with her wings flung wide open and her beak aimed straight at him.
questions.explanationZh: 突然！一隻紅母雞張開翅膀，「呼」地一下衝過來，尖嘴對準他！嚇得灰鴨寶寶退了好幾步。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: From every side of the yard, sharp beaks came toward him fast.
questions.question: What did the farmyard animals do?
questions.explanationZh: 四面八方的尖嘴一起衝過來——這不是玩，這是「攻擊」！可憐的灰寶寶。
questions.sentenceZh: 農場四面八方，一張張尖嘴飛快地往他衝過來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: From every side of the yard, sharp beaks came toward him fast.
questions.sentenceZh: 農場四面八方，一張張尖嘴飛快地往他衝過來。
questions.questionEn: Did only one animal attack him?
questions.explanationZh: 「四面八方」！不只一隻，是好多好多隻尖嘴一起衝過來，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: At first, mother duck stood between him and the hens.
questions.sentenceEasy: Mother duck stood in front of him.
questions.sentenceHard: At first, mother duck bravely placed herself between her grey son and the attacking hens, shielding him with her own body.
questions.explanationZh: 一開始，鴨媽媽勇敢地擋在他前面，用自己的身體把那些兇巴巴的母雞擋下來，不讓她們靠近她的灰兒子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She stepped close so the hens could not reach her grey son.
questions.question: What did mother duck do at first?
questions.explanationZh: 鴨媽媽把自己擋在前面，母雞碰不到兒子——這就是「保護他」。媽媽的愛，就是這樣啊。
questions.sentenceZh: 她靠近他，讓母雞們無法觸碰到她的灰色兒子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: At first, mother duck stood between him and the hens. But then she heard the other animals laugh out loud.
questions.sentenceZh: 一開始，鴨媽媽擋在他和母雞們之間。但接著她聽到其他動物大聲笑了起來。
questions.question: What changed when the animals laughed?
options: mother duck began to step back
optionsZh: 鴨媽媽更努力保護他了
questions.explanationZh: 故事說鴨媽媽「聽到笑聲，眼神就變了」——那笑聲讓她開始動搖，開始退縮。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But then she heard the other animals laugh out loud.
questions.sentenceEasy: Then the animals laughed at him.
questions.sentenceHard: But then the laughter of the other animals rang out across the yard, and something in mother duck seemed to change.
questions.explanationZh: 但是那一陣笑聲傳過來，嘰嘰嘎嘎的，大聲又刺耳——鴨媽媽的眼神，慢慢地變了……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then she turned her head, and did not say a word.
questions.sentenceZh: 然後她轉過頭去，一個字也沒說。
questions.question: What did mother duck do at the end?
options: she pushed him away with hard words
the hen offered him warm food
optionsZh: 大家看到他都笑著迎接他
她用很傷人的話把他推走
那隻母雞給了他溫暖的食物
questions.explanationZh: 鴨媽媽把頭轉開，一個字也沒說——就是「沉默地轉身走掉」，那個背影，讓灰寶寶的心涼了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did the grey duckling feel now?
questions.question: How did he feel?
questions.explanationZh: 被啄、被追、被嘲笑，還沒有人站在他這邊——這麼多人欺負他，只剩下「孤單」了。
questions.sentenceZh: 灰色小鴨現在的心情是什麼？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these farmyard phrases.
questions.sentenceZh: 把農場裡的這些片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 農場那一段出現的四組詞，你還記得嗎？配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Soon the family walked to the farmyard for the first time.
questions.sentenceZh: 很快地，全家第一次一起走向農場了。
questions.questionEn: Had the grey duckling been to the farmyard before?
questions.explanationZh: 「第一次走向農場」——這是第一次，之前從來沒去過，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: There were big hens, loud ducks, and an angry old goose in the farmyard.
questions.sentenceZh: 農場裡有大母雞、嘎嘎叫的鴨子，和一隻兇巴巴的老鵝。
questions.question: What was the farmyard like when they arrived?
optionsZh: 擠滿了很多吵鬧的動物
questions.explanationZh: 大母雞、嘎嘎叫的鴨、兇老鵝——農場裡「擠滿了很多吵鬧的動物」，熱鬧得很。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then she turned her head, and did not say a word.
questions.sentenceZh: 然後她轉過頭去，一個字也沒說。
questions.questionEn: Did mother duck speak up to defend the grey duckling at the end?
questions.explanationZh: 她「轉過頭、一個字也沒說」——她什麼都沒說，沒有替他說話，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She stepped close so the hens could not reach her grey son.
questions.sentenceZh: 她靠近他，讓母雞們無法碰到她的灰色兒子。
questions.question: What did mother duck do to protect him?
options: she had never felt so much pain
everything was easy for her now
the cat stayed close to comfort her
her wings grew stronger that day
optionsZh: 現在對她來說一切都很容易
那隻貓靠近她給她安慰
那天她的翅膀變得更有力
questions.explanationZh: 「靠近、讓母雞碰不到他」——她是「站在他和母雞之間」擋著。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then she turned her head, and did not say a word...
questions.sentenceEasy: She turned away and said nothing.
questions.sentenceHard: Then, slowly, mother duck turned her head away from him and did not speak a single word...
questions.explanationZh: 然後，鴨媽媽慢慢把頭轉開了，一個字也沒說……那個背影，讓灰寶寶的心涼了一半。

storyBeat: 逃跑 — 野鴨朋友 — 槍聲

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 run = 跑
🔑 wild = 野生
🔑 rest = 休息
🔑 grass = 草
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: That night, he climbed over the fence and left the farmyard.
questions.sentenceEasy: He ran away from the farmyard that night.
questions.sentenceHard: Under the cover of night, he quietly climbed over the fence and slipped away from the farmyard forever.
questions.explanationZh: 夜深了，他悄悄爬過籬笆，一個人走進黑暗裡。農場的燈光越來越遠，越來越小……他走了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He walked across cold fields all the way to a quiet marsh.
questions.sentenceEasy: He walked to a quiet marsh.
questions.sentenceHard: He trudged alone across cold, dark fields, walking on and on until he reached a still and quiet marsh at last.
questions.explanationZh: 他一個人穿過又冷又黑的田野，腳踩在硬硬的土地上，走啊走啊，走到一片安靜的沼澤才停下來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His small feet ached after many hours on the rough ground.
questions.questionEn: Was his journey short and easy?
questions.explanationZh: 腳都走到痠了，還走了好幾個小時——這段路又長又辛苦，哪裡短又容易？所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 走了好幾個小時，他小小的腳已經又痠又痛。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He climbed over the fence at night and walked across cold fields to a quiet marsh.
questions.sentenceZh: 他趁夜爬過籬笆，穿過冰冷的田野，走到一片安靜的沼澤。
questions.question: Why did the grey duckling leave the farmyard at night?
options: he wanted to find more food
he could not stand the cruelty anymore
he heard a beautiful sound from outside
mother duck told him to leave
optionsZh: 他再也受不了那些欺負
他聽到外面有美麗的聲音
questions.explanationZh: 被啄、被追、被媽媽轉頭——再也受不了了，只好趁夜溜走，去找一個不被欺負的地方。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Two wild ducks let him rest beside them in the tall grass.
questions.sentenceEasy: Two wild ducks let him rest with them.
questions.sentenceHard: Two wild ducks, resting among the tall reeds, allowed him to settle beside them without chasing him away.
questions.explanationZh: 兩隻野鴨窩在高高的草叢裡，看了看他，沒有追他走，讓他在旁邊安靜地休息。這是他第一次，有人不趕他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Two wild ducks let him rest beside them in the tall grass.
questions.sentenceZh: 兩隻野鴨讓他在高高的草叢裡，靠在他們旁邊休息。
questions.questionEn: Did the wild ducks chase him away?
questions.explanationZh: 他們讓他靠著休息——沒有趕他走，是第一次有動物不趕他！所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One of them shared half his lunch and would not leave his side.
questions.question: What did one wild duck become to him?
questions.explanationZh: 分了一半的飯、一步也不離開——這不是老師，不是哥哥，就是「朋友」啊！
questions.sentenceZh: 其中一隻把自己的午餐分了一半給他，還一路陪在他身邊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: For one bright day, he did not feel so alone anymore.
questions.sentenceEasy: That day, he felt less alone.
questions.sentenceHard: For one warm and golden day, beside a friend who did not mock him, he no longer felt the heavy weight of loneliness.
questions.explanationZh: 有朋友陪在身邊的那一天，陽光曬在身上暖暖的，他覺得那股一直壓著心頭的孤單，輕了一點點。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: For one bright day, he did not feel so alone anymore. The warm sun made his grey feathers feel almost soft.
questions.sentenceZh: 有一整天，他覺得不那麼孤單了。溫暖的太陽讓他的灰羽毛感覺幾乎變軟了。
questions.question: What made the grey duckling feel a little better by the marsh?
options: he had no strength to move
the snow melted around his feet
a warm fire kept him going
optionsZh: 他的翅膀帶著他飛得很遠
questions.explanationZh: 有朋友陪、有暖陽曬——就這兩樣，讓那一天的心情好了一點點。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The warm sun made his grey feathers feel almost soft.
questions.question: How did he feel that day?
questions.explanationZh: 有朋友、有暖陽——心情當然「好一點」了。雖然還不到開心，但至少不那麼沉重了。
questions.sentenceZh: 暖暖的太陽，讓他的灰羽毛感覺幾乎柔軟了起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But then loud cracks came from the trees by the water.
questions.sentenceEasy: Loud sounds came from the trees.
questions.sentenceHard: But then a series of sharp, thunderous cracks shattered the peace, echoing out from the trees along the water's edge.
questions.explanationZh: 就在這時候，「砰！砰！砰！」——水邊的樹叢裡傳來響亮的爆裂聲，像是晴天霹靂，把一切都打碎了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But then loud cracks came from the trees by the water. His friend dropped to the ground.
questions.sentenceZh: 但是接著，水邊的樹叢傳來響亮的爆裂聲。他的朋友倒在地上了。
questions.question: What happened to his wild duck friend?
options: he could die alone in the cold
his friends came to rescue him
warm spring arrived just in time
optionsZh: 他可能會在寒冷中孤獨死去
農夫給了他一個避風的地方
溫暖的春天剛好及時到來
questions.explanationZh: 槍聲一響，他的朋友「咚」地倒下去了——「倒在地上」就是答案。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What was the loud sound?
questions.question: What was the sound?
questions.explanationZh: 響亮的爆裂聲從樹叢裡傳出來，又突然又嚇人——這就是「槍聲」！獵人來了。
questions.sentenceZh: 那個響亮的聲音是什麼？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these phrases from the escape scene.
questions.sentenceZh: 把逃跑那一段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是這一節的關鍵片語，配一配記得更清楚！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: That night, he climbed over the fence and left the farmyard.
questions.sentenceZh: 那天夜裡，他爬過籬笆，離開了農場。
questions.question: When did the grey duckling escape?
options: right after the hens attacked him
questions.explanationZh: 「That night」——就是那天夜裡，趁著黑暗溜走的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He walked across cold fields all the way to a quiet marsh.
questions.sentenceZh: 他穿過寒冷的田野，一路走到一片安靜的沼澤。
questions.questionEn: Was the marsh loud and busy like the farmyard?
questions.explanationZh: 沼澤是「安靜」的——和農場的嘎嘎叫聲完全不同，答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Two wild ducks let him rest beside them in the tall grass.
questions.sentenceZh: 兩隻野鴨讓他在高高的草叢裡，靠在他們旁邊休息。
questions.question: Where did the grey duckling rest?
options: in tall grass beside wild ducks
in a warm nest by the pond here
under a big tree in the woods
optionsZh: 在野鴨旁邊的高草叢裡
questions.explanationZh: 「兩隻野鴨讓他在高高的草叢裡靠在他們旁邊休息」——就是「在野鴨旁邊的高草叢裡」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: For one bright day, he did not feel so alone anymore.
questions.sentenceZh: 有一整天，他覺得不那麼孤單了。
questions.question: How long did his happiness by the marsh last?
optionsZh: 整整三個漫長的黑暗星期
questions.explanationZh: 「For one bright day」——只有一天，短短的快樂，然後槍聲就打破了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His friend dropped to the ground. He ran with his heart pounding...
questions.sentenceEasy: His friend fell down. He ran away fast.
questions.sentenceHard: His friend crumpled to the ground without a sound, and he fled into the reeds with his heart hammering wildly in his chest...
questions.explanationZh: 他的朋友「咚」地倒在地上，一動也不動。他嚇得拔腿就跑，心臟砰砰砰地跳個不停，跑進蘆葦叢裡……

storyBeat: 小屋 — 貓和母雞嘲笑 — 走入冷夜

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 cottage = 小屋
🔑 cat = 貓
🔑 fire = 火
🔑 claw = 爪子
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: After many days, he found a small cottage in the woods.
questions.sentenceEasy: He found a small cottage in the woods.
questions.sentenceHard: After many weary days of wandering alone, he stumbled upon a small, smoke-warmed cottage tucked deep in the woods.
questions.explanationZh: 走了好多好多天，他又累又餓，突然在樹林深處，看見一間小小的屋子，煙囪還冒著煙，暖呼呼的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: An old woman lived there with her cat and her hen.
questions.sentenceEasy: An old woman had a cat and a hen.
questions.sentenceHard: Inside the cottage lived an old woman, along with her sleek cat who purred by the fire and her proud hen who ruled the kitchen.
questions.explanationZh: 屋子裡住著一個老婆婆，還有一隻在爐火邊咕嚕咕嚕叫的貓，和一隻抬頭挺胸、很有架子的母雞。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The cottage door creaked open when he gave it a soft push.
questions.explanationZh: 輕輕一推就開——門根本沒鎖緊，所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 他輕輕推了一下小屋的門，門「嘎吱」一聲就開了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: After many days, he found a small cottage in the woods. An old woman kept him in her warm kitchen.
questions.sentenceZh: 走了好多天之後，他在樹林裡找到一間小屋。一位老婆婆把他留在溫暖的廚房裡。
questions.question: Where did the grey duckling find shelter after many days?
options: in a barn by a river
under a big rock by the road
in an old woman's warm kitchen
in a duck pond in the forest
optionsZh: 在路邊一塊大石頭下面
在一個老婦人溫暖的廚房裡
questions.explanationZh: 他走了好多天，走到一間小屋，老婆婆把他留在廚房裡取暖——這就是他找到的避風港。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The old woman kept him in her warm kitchen for many weeks.
questions.sentenceEasy: He stayed in the warm kitchen.
questions.sentenceHard: The old woman, taking pity on the cold and tired creature, kept him sheltered in her warm kitchen for many weeks.
questions.explanationZh: 老婆婆見他又冷又可憐，就把他留在溫暖的廚房裡，一住就是好幾個禮拜。那是他好久以來最暖的時光。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The cat sat heavy on his back and dug into his soft feathers.
questions.question: How did the cat treat him?
questions.explanationZh: 貓重重坐上去、爪子往羽毛裡抓——這哪是玩，是「用爪子壓他」呀，又痛又委屈。
questions.sentenceZh: 那隻貓重重地坐在他背上，爪子陷進他柔軟的羽毛裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The cat sat heavy on his back and dug into his soft feathers.
questions.sentenceZh: 那隻貓重重地坐在他背上，爪子陷進他柔軟的羽毛裡。
questions.questionEn: Was the cat friendly to the grey duckling?
questions.explanationZh: 重重坐上去、爪子往羽毛裡抓——這不是友善，是欺負他！所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hen counted her own warm eggs each morning and looked away.
questions.sentenceEasy: The hen counted her eggs and looked away.
questions.sentenceHard: Each morning, the hen carefully counted her own warm eggs, then turned her head away without a single glance in his direction.
questions.explanationZh: 每天早上，母雞數完自己的蛋，就把頭轉開，眼睛都不看他一眼——那種冷淡，比爪子更刺人。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She told him he was useless and not worth the food he ate.
questions.question: Why did the hen mock him?
questions.explanationZh: 母雞覺得自己會生蛋，很有用；他不會生蛋，所以嫌他「沒用、不值得吃飯」，答案是「他不會生蛋」。
questions.sentenceZh: 她告訴他說，他沒有用，不值得吃那麼多飯。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The cat pressed claws on him, and the hen told him he was useless because he never gave any eggs.
questions.sentenceZh: 貓用爪子壓他，母雞告訴他說他沒用，因為他不會生蛋。
questions.question: How did the cat and the hen treat the grey duckling?
options: with gentle feathers close around him
a cold empty space all around
optionsZh: 柔軟的羽毛緊緊圍著他
questions.explanationZh: 爪子壓他是「殘忍」，說他沒用是「嘲笑」——合起來就是「殘忍地嘲笑他」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The cat asked him if he could purr by the warm fire.
questions.sentenceEasy: The cat asked if he could purr.
questions.sentenceHard: The cat stretched lazily by the warm fire and asked him, with a slow blink, whether he was capable of purring as she did.
questions.explanationZh: 貓懶洋洋地靠在火邊，慢吞吞地問他：「你會咕嚕咕嚕叫嗎？」他搖搖頭——他根本做不到這個。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The cat asked him if he could purr by the warm fire.
questions.sentenceZh: 貓問他，你會在爐火邊發出咕嚕咕嚕的聲音嗎？
questions.question: What did the cat want to know?
options: whether he had any friends left
if spring would ever come back
the swans might chase him away
optionsZh: 是不是有人跟著他來這裡
天鵝們會不會把他趕走
questions.explanationZh: 貓問的是「你會像我一樣咕嚕咕嚕叫嗎？」——她想知道他會不會「咕嚕叫」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wind under the door felt sharper than the cat's claws on his back.
questions.question: What is waiting outside?
questions.explanationZh: 門縫的風比貓爪還刺——外面一定又黑又冷，所以等著他的是「冷夜」。
questions.sentenceZh: 從門縫鑽進來的風，比貓爪子刮在背上還刺。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these phrases from the cottage scene.
questions.sentenceZh: 把小屋那一段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是這節重要的片語，配對看看！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: After many days, he found a small cottage in the woods.
questions.sentenceZh: 走了好多天之後，他在樹林裡找到一間小屋。
questions.question: How long did the grey duckling walk before finding the cottage?
optionsZh: 一個人待了好幾個禮拜
questions.explanationZh: 「After many days」——走了好多天才找到小屋，不是一下子就到的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: An old woman lived there with her cat and her hen.
questions.sentenceZh: 一個老婆婆住在那裡，跟她的貓和母雞在一起。
questions.questionEn: Did the old woman live alone in the cottage?
questions.explanationZh: 她有一隻貓和一隻母雞——不是「獨居」，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The old woman kept him in her warm kitchen for many weeks.
questions.sentenceZh: 老婆婆把他留在她溫暖的廚房裡好幾個禮拜。
questions.question: Where did the grey duckling stay in the cottage?
options: with a warm friend close by
in the cold, without any hope
optionsZh: 身邊有溫暖的朋友陪著
在寒冷中，沒有任何希望
questions.explanationZh: 「kept him in her warm kitchen」——老婆婆把他留在廚房裡，暖暖的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "What good are you?" they asked. He pushed open the door and walked into the cold.
questions.sentenceZh: 「你有什麼用？」他們問。他推開門走進了冷夜裡。
questions.question: What did the grey duckling do when the cat and hen mocked him?
options: he now had hope of finding more
all the pain was gone forever
mother duck came back for him
optionsZh: 他現在有希望找到更多同伴了
所有的傷痛都永遠消失了
questions.explanationZh: 「推開門、走進了冷夜」——他沒有留下，沒有哭，就這樣安靜地走出去了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "What good are you?" they asked. He pushed open the door and walked into the cold...
questions.sentenceEasy: "What good are you?" He left and went into the cold.
questions.sentenceHard: "What good are you to anyone?" they demanded. With a quiet sadness, he pushed open the cottage door and stepped alone into the bitter cold...
questions.explanationZh: 「你有什麼用？」他們說。他沒有回答，只是輕輕推開門，一步走進了冰冷的黑夜裡……又是一個人了。

storyBeat: 冬天 — 白鳥南飛 — 困在冰中 — 農夫救起

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 winter = 冬天
🔑 ice = 冰
🔑 white = 白色
🔑 farmer = 農夫
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In the autumn sky, he saw great white birds flying high.
questions.sentenceEasy: He saw big white birds in the sky.
questions.sentenceHard: One crisp autumn evening, he looked up and saw a flock of great white birds soaring high across the fading sky.
questions.explanationZh: 秋天的黃昏，他抬起頭，看見一群大白鳥劃過天空，那麼高、那麼美，像夢一樣飛過去了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their long necks stretched south as they crossed the clouds.
questions.sentenceEasy: They flew south with long necks.
questions.sentenceHard: With their long graceful necks extended and their powerful wings beating steadily, the great birds swept southward through the clouds.
questions.explanationZh: 白鳥們長長的脖子向南伸著，翅膀穩穩地一下一下拍著雲，越飛越遠……他的眼睛一路跟著看，捨不得移開。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He watched them until his neck grew sore from looking up.
questions.questionEn: Did he look at the white birds for a long time?
questions.explanationZh: 脖子看到痠了，才捨得停下來——這麼久才放棄，當然是「看很久」，答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 他一直仰著頭看，看到脖子都痠了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In the autumn sky, he saw great white birds flying high with long necks stretched south.
questions.sentenceZh: 在秋天的天空，他看見一群脖子很長的大白鳥飛得好高，朝著南方飛去。
questions.question: What did the grey duckling see in the autumn sky?
options: huge white birds soaring in a line
a grey goose near the pond
tiny brown sparrows in the grass
optionsZh: 一排大大的白鳥在天上翱翔
questions.explanationZh: 秋天的天空，他看見長脖子的大白鳥飛過去，朝著南方——就是「大白鳥朝南方飛去」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Soon snow fell, and the pond turned into hard, shining glass.
questions.sentenceEasy: Snow came. The pond turned to ice.
questions.sentenceHard: Soon the first snowflakes drifted down, and within days the pond had frozen into a hard, gleaming sheet of ice.
questions.explanationZh: 雪花輕輕飄下來，池塘慢慢結冰，最後變成了一片亮晶晶、硬梆梆的大鏡子。冬天來了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Soon snow fell, and the pond turned into hard, shining glass.
questions.sentenceZh: 很快地，雪下了，池塘變成了硬硬的、閃亮的玻璃。
questions.questionEn: Did the pond stay liquid after the snow came?
questions.explanationZh: 池塘「變成硬梆梆的亮玻璃」——這就是冰！水已經凍住了，不是液體了，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His feet would not move, and the water around him had grown hard.
questions.question: What happened to him in the pond?
questions.explanationZh: 腳動不了、水變硬——冰把他整個卡住了，這就是「被困在冰裡」。太危險了！
questions.sentenceZh: 他的腳完全動不了，周圍的水已經凍得硬梆梆了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A farmer came across the field and broke the ice apart.
questions.sentenceEasy: A farmer came and broke the ice.
questions.sentenceHard: A farmer crossing the frozen field noticed the trapped bird and came over to break the ice apart with his heavy boots.
questions.explanationZh: 一個農夫走過結冰的田野，發現他被卡住，就彎下身，用力把冰打開，把他救出來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A farmer came across the field and ___ the ice apart.
questions.sentenceZh: 一個農夫走過田野，把冰打開了。
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh: 這是已經發生的事，所以用過去式。break 的過去式是 broke！「The farmer broke the ice apart」——農夫把冰打開了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He lifted the shaking bird and walked back toward his cottage.
questions.question: What did the farmer do?
questions.explanationZh: 抱起他、走回家——農夫把他帶回去照顧，就是「帶他回家」。
questions.sentenceZh: 農夫把那隻抖個不停的鳥抱起來，往自己的小屋走去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The farmer's small children came running to see the new bird.
questions.sentenceEasy: The children ran to see him.
questions.sentenceHard: The farmer's young children, curious and excited, came running from inside the house to crowd around the strange new bird.
questions.explanationZh: 農夫的小孩聽到有新奇的鳥，立刻從屋裡衝出來，又喊又跑地圍過去——那些小小的手，讓他嚇壞了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He lifted the shaking bird and walked back toward his cottage.
questions.sentenceZh: 他把那隻抖個不停的鳥抱起來，往自己的小屋走去。
questions.questionEn: Did the farmer help the grey duckling?
questions.explanationZh: 農夫抱起他、帶他回家——這當然是「幫助他」了，答 Yes！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He had never been close to people, and small hands reached for him.
questions.question: How did he feel when the children ran to him?
questions.explanationZh: 從來沒跟人靠近過，突然一群小手伸過來——他當然「害怕」！就算人家是好意，也嚇死了。
questions.sentenceZh: 他從來沒有靠近過人，小小的手向他伸過來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these phrases from the winter and farmer scene.
questions.sentenceZh: 把冬天和農夫那一段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是這節故事裡的重要片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In the autumn sky, he saw great white birds flying high.
questions.sentenceZh: 在秋天的天空，他看見大白鳥飛得好高好高。
questions.question: When did the grey duckling first see the great white birds?
questions.explanationZh: 「In the autumn sky」——是在秋天，天上看見的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His feet would not move, and the water around him had grown hard.
questions.sentenceZh: 他的腳完全動不了，周圍的水已經凍得硬梆梆了。
questions.questionEn: Could the grey duckling move freely in the frozen pond?
questions.explanationZh: 腳動不了、水凍硬了——他完全動不了，哪裡「自由移動」？答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He flapped his wings and ran out into the deep, white snow again.
questions.sentenceZh: 他用力拍動翅膀，再次衝出去，跑進又深又白的雪裡。
questions.question: What did the grey duckling do when the children reached for him?
options: the birds flew away and left him
he finally saw what he truly was
mother duck came back at last
all the animals cheered for him
optionsZh: 鳥兒們飛走、把他留在那裡
他終於看清楚自己真正的樣子了
questions.explanationZh: 「拍動翅膀、衝進深雪裡」——他嚇壞了，逃進雪裡了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The farmer's small children came running to see the new bird. He flapped his wings and ran out into the deep, white snow again.
questions.sentenceZh: 農夫的小孩跑過來看這隻新來的鳥。他用力拍翅膀，再次跑進又深又白的雪裡。
questions.question: Why did the grey duckling run away from the farmer's house?
options: the long wait was finally worth it
his sadness would never go away
nothing ever really changes in life
beauty is the only thing that matters
optionsZh: 漫長的等待終於值得了
他的悲傷永遠不會消失
生命中什麼都不會真正改變
美麗是世界上唯一重要的事
questions.explanationZh: 「從沒靠近過人，突然小手伸過來」——是孩子們的動作嚇到他，讓他拔腿就跑。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He flapped his wings and ran out into the deep, white snow again...
questions.sentenceEasy: He ran back out into the snow.
questions.sentenceHard: Overwhelmed with fear, he beat his wings against the air and burst out through the door, disappearing once more into the deep, silent snow...
questions.explanationZh: 他用力拍動翅膀，「嘩」地一下衝出門，再次消失進又深又白的雪裡……不知道要去哪裡，只知道要跑。

storyBeat: 春天 — 倒影 — 天鵝 — 美麗

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚故事的新單字，左邊中文、右邊英文：
🔑 spring = 春天
🔑 reflection = 倒影
🔑 swan = 天鵝
🔑 beautiful = 美麗
這 4 個字記住了，故事一開口就聽得懂！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Spring came at last, and the cold ice melted away.
questions.sentenceEasy: Spring came. The ice melted.
questions.sentenceHard: After the long and bitter winter, spring arrived at last and the cold ice slowly melted away into the warming water.
questions.explanationZh: 漫長的冬天終於過去了！冰慢慢融化，水流輕輕流動，春天回來了，帶著暖暖的氣息。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Green leaves opened, and warm sun shone over the pond.
questions.sentenceEasy: Green leaves grew. The sun was warm.
questions.sentenceHard: Fresh green leaves unfurled on every branch, and warm golden sunlight spread gently across the still surface of the pond.
questions.explanationZh: 葉子一片一片從枝頭展開，金色的陽光灑在池塘上，波光粼粼，像撒了一把碎金子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Soft new flowers grew along the edge of the quiet water.
questions.questionEn: Is it still cold winter now?
questions.explanationZh: 新花開了、暖陽曬著——這明明是春天的樣子，哪裡是「冬天」？所以答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 水邊長出了嬌嫩的新花，靜靜地開著。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Spring came at last. The cold ice melted, green leaves opened, and warm sun shone over the pond.
questions.sentenceZh: 春天終於來了。冰融化了，綠葉展開，暖陽灑在池塘上。
questions.question: What signs show that spring has arrived?
options: snow is falling and ice is growing
the wild ducks flew away south again
the hens are returning to the farmyard
leaves grow and ice melts under warm sun
optionsZh: 葉子長出來，冰在暖陽下融化
questions.explanationZh: 冰融了、葉子展開、陽光暖暖的——這些加在一起就是春天的模樣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He saw the same white birds gliding across the pond.
questions.sentenceEasy: He saw the white birds on the pond.
questions.sentenceHard: There on the shimmering pond, he caught sight of the very same great white birds he had seen in the autumn sky, now gliding serenely across the water.
questions.explanationZh: 就是那群白鳥！他在秋天看過牠們飛走，現在牠們回來了，輕輕滑過亮晶晶的池塘。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted very much to be near them, even if they would chase him.
questions.question: How did he feel toward the white birds?
questions.explanationZh: 就算知道可能被追走，還是非常想靠近——這就是「被吸引」，心裡有股說不清的牽引。
questions.sentenceZh: 他非常想靠近他們，就算他們會追他走，他也想試試看。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted very much to be near them, even if they would chase him.
questions.sentenceZh: 他非常想靠近他們，就算他們會追他走，他也想試試看。
questions.questionEn: Was the grey duckling afraid to approach the white birds?
questions.explanationZh: 他說「就算被追走也想靠近」——他知道可能會被趕，所以是「害怕但還是想靠近」。有一點害怕，所以答 Yes。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He lowered his head to the water, ready to give up.
questions.sentenceEasy: He looked down at the water and gave up.
questions.sentenceHard: With a heavy heart, he bowed his head down toward the still water, convinced at last that there was nothing left to hope for.
questions.explanationZh: 他低下頭，望著水面，心想：反正那麼醜，什麼都沒有了。他準備好了——就這樣放棄吧……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But the still pond showed him a face he did not know.
questions.question: What did he see in the water?
options: only a small grey baby bird
one bird with a long neck
just a fish in the water
optionsZh: 只有一隻灰色的小幼鳥
questions.explanationZh: 水面倒影出一張陌生的臉，長長的脖子、大大的翅膀——那是天鵝的臉，不是醜小鴨了！
questions.sentenceZh: 但平靜的池水照出了一張他不認識的臉。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He lowered his head to the water, ready to give up. But the still pond showed him a face he did not know — a long white neck and bright wings.
questions.sentenceZh: 他低下頭，準備放棄了。但平靜的池水照出了一張他不認識的臉——長長的白脖子和閃亮的翅膀。
questions.question: What did the grey duckling discover when he looked in the water?
options: he bent his neck low in thanks
his wings spread wide with joy
the swans sang a welcome song
optionsZh: 鴨媽媽呼喚著他的名字
天鵝們唱了一首歡迎歌
questions.explanationZh: 水面倒影出長長的白脖子、閃亮的翅膀——那是一隻天鵝！他看見的是「一隻美麗的天鵝回望著他」，那就是他自己。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A long white neck and bright wings looked back at him.
questions.sentenceEasy: He saw a white neck and big wings in the water.
questions.sentenceHard: Gazing back at him from the mirror of the pond was a creature with a long, elegant white neck and broad, shining wings.
questions.explanationZh: 水面像一面鏡子，映出了長長的白脖子和閃亮的翅膀。那……是他自己嗎？他不敢相信。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the phrases from the Ugly Duckling's story.
questions.explanationZh: 把醜小鴨故事裡的重要片語配配看！左邊中文、右邊英文，你認識這些了嗎？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Children by the pond called him the most beautiful of all. He had been a swan all along.
questions.sentenceZh: 池邊的孩子說他是最美的。他一直都是天鵝。
questions.question: What is the main message of the Ugly Duckling story?
options: staying in your nest is wisest
fast animals always win in the end
your real place may still be ahead
optionsZh: 待在自己的窩裡最聰明
速度快的動物最後都會贏
你真正的地方也許還在前方
questions.explanationZh: 他從一開始就是天鵝，只是還沒找到自己的地方——故事的訊息是：你的家或許還在前方等你，只要走下去就會找到。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match these phrases from the swan ending.
questions.sentenceZh: 把天鵝結尾那一段的片語配起來！
questions.explanationZh: 這四組都是這節最精彩的片語，配一配！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Green leaves opened, and warm sun shone over the pond.
questions.sentenceZh: 綠葉展開了，暖陽灑在池塘上。
questions.question: Which season does this describe?
questions.explanationZh: 綠葉、暖陽、冰融化——這些全都是「春天」的訊號！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He lowered his head to the water, ready to give up.
questions.sentenceZh: 他低下頭，對著水面，準備放棄了。
questions.questionEn: Was the grey duckling feeling hopeful just before he looked in the water?
questions.explanationZh: 「準備放棄了」——他已經沒有希望了，不是充滿希望，所以答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A long white neck and bright wings looked back at him.
questions.sentenceZh: 長長的白脖子和閃亮的翅膀，在水面回望著他。
questions.question: What kind of bird did he see in his reflection?
options: a beautiful white swan at last
optionsZh: 終於是一隻美麗的白天鵝
還是跟以前一樣只是隻鴨子
跟往常一樣小小的灰灰的
questions.explanationZh: 長白脖子、亮翅膀——這是天鵝的樣子！水面倒影出的是「一隻美麗的天鵝」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Children by the pond called him the most beautiful of all. He had been a swan all along.
questions.sentenceZh: 池邊的孩子說他是最美的。他一直都是天鵝。
questions.question: What did the children say about him?
options: they chose him as their own
optionsZh: 那個溫暖的日子什麼都沒改變
questions.explanationZh: 「called him the most beautiful of all」——孩子們說他是最美的！那一刻，他才真正相信了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Children by the pond called him the most beautiful of all. He had been a swan all along...
questions.sentenceEasy: The children said he was beautiful. He was a swan.
questions.sentenceHard: The children gathered by the water's edge and declared him the most beautiful bird they had ever seen — for he had always been a swan, all along...
questions.explanationZh: 池邊的孩子齊聲說：「你是最美的！」他終於明白了——原來他一直都是天鵝，從來就不是醜小鴨……
